name: Buddhist Mythology
description: The sacred abode of Avalokiteshvara and historic residence of the Dalai Lama in Lhasa, Tibet. Named after Mount Potalaka, the mythical abode of Avalokiteshvara.

keyMyths.title: The Legend of Avalokiteshvara's Origin
keyMyths.description: According to Buddhist lore, Avalokiteshvara (Chenrezig in Tibetan) arose from a ray of light emitted by Amitabha Buddha. He embodies compassion and vows to liberate all beings from suffering, delaying his own Buddhahood until all others are saved.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the World by Brahma
keyMyths.description: While Buddhism does not ascribe to a single creator god like Brahma in Hinduism, some early Buddhist texts acknowledge Brahma as a powerful deity. However, Buddhism reinterprets Brahma's role, suggesting that he mistakenly believes himself to be the sole creator due to his initial awakening in an empty universe.

keyMyths.title: The Jataka Tales
keyMyths.description: The Jataka tales recount the previous lives of the Buddha in both human and animal forms. These stories illustrate the development of the bodhisattva's virtues, such as generosity, wisdom, and compassion, through countless lifetimes of selfless action.

keyMyths.title: Mount Meru and the Buddhist Cosmos
keyMyths.description: Mount Meru stands as the central axis of the Buddhist cosmos, surrounded by continents, oceans, and celestial realms. This cosmological model, shared with Hinduism and Jainism, depicts a hierarchical structure with various realms of existence influenced by karma and rebirth.

keyMyths.title: The Story of Mara's Temptation
keyMyths.description: Mara, the embodiment of temptation and death, attempts to prevent Siddhartha Gautama from achieving enlightenment. He sends his daughters to seduce Siddhartha, unleashes armies of demons, and challenges his right to sit beneath the Bodhi tree, but Siddhartha resists all temptations and attains Buddhahood.

sources.name: Karandavyuha Sutra
sources.description: A Mahayana Buddhist sutra that prominently features the bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara.
sources.title: Karandavyuha Sutra

sources.name: Digha Nikaya, Kevatta Sutta
sources.description: A sutta from the Digha Nikaya of the Pali Canon that discusses the role and limitations of Brahma.
sources.title: Digha Nikaya, Kevatta Sutta

sources.name: Jataka Tales (Pali Canon)
sources.description: A collection of stories in the Pali Canon describing the previous lives of the Buddha.
sources.title: Jataka Tales (Pali Canon)

sources.name: Abhidharma-kosa
sources.description: A comprehensive text of Abhidharma philosophy by Vasubandhu, detailing Buddhist cosmology and psychology.
sources.title: Abhidharma-kosa

sources.name: Lalitavistara Sutra
sources.description: A Mahayana Buddhist sutra that narrates the life of the Buddha, from his birth to his first sermon.
sources.title: Lalitavistara Sutra
